description: Template defining the structured report each subagent must return. The primary agent parses these reports for synthesis.

reportFields.label: Approach
reportFields.description: The name of the approach being explored.

reportFields.label: Core Idea
reportFields.description: 1-3 sentences. The key algorithmic insight. Why does this work?

reportFields.label: Walkthrough
reportFields.description: Step-by-step trace through the algorithm on a concrete small example.

subfields.label: Example input
subfields.description: Concrete small input to trace through.

subfields.label: Steps
subfields.description: Step-by-step trace showing intermediate state at each meaningful step. Be specific: show what data structure looks like, what values change, etc.

subfields.label: Result
subfields.description: Expected output and confirmation it's correct.

reportFields.label: Complexity
reportFields.description: Time, space, and optionally preprocessing complexity with justifications.

reportFields.label: Edge Cases
reportFields.description: At least 3 genuinely distinct edge cases with explanations of how the approach handles each.

reportFields.label: Implementation Considerations
reportFields.description: Non-obvious choices a developer would actually trip over. Minimum 3.

reportFields.label: Fit Score (self-assessed)
reportFields.description: The subagent's self-assessment of how well the approach fits the problem's specific constraints.

subfields.label: Reason
subfields.description: 1 sentence — how well does this fit the problem's specific constraints?

synthesisNotes: The Fit Score is the subagent's self-assessment. Treat it as a signal, not ground truth. A subagent assigned to explore an approach will naturally find reasons it works. Weight your own synthesis over the self-assessed score.
If a subagent returns a malformed or incomplete report, note the gap in your synthesis output rather than silently patching it. Missing sections are meaningful data.
